grading.extracted_answer: To solve the problem, follow this two-step algorithmic approach: 1. Spatial Partitioning: Use a spatial partitioning engine (such as a k-d tree or a Voronoi-based rectangular decomposition) to generate an initial set of non-overlapping axis-aligned rectangles {Ri} such that each rectangle Ri contains exactly one requested point Pi. 2. Area Optimization: Pass the initial rectangles and the target areas {Ai} to an area optimization refiner. This refiner uses an iterative greedy scaling and clipping algorithm. For each rectangle, attempt to scale its dimensions to match the target area Ai while maintaining the center/point Pi within its bounds. If a scaling operation causes an overlap with a neighbor, clip the boundary of the expanding rectangle to the edge of the neighbor. This process is repeated iteratively until convergence or a maximum iteration count is reached, ensuring all rectangles remain disjoint while minimizing the total area error sum |Area(R'i) - Ai|.

Question ID: coding-S1-ahc001-atcoder-ad
Title: S1. AHC001 / AtCoder Ad
Family: coding
Entry type: coding

  "grader_type": "continuous_score"
}

Problem:
Place many non-overlapping axis-aligned ad rectangles in a 10000 x 10000 square so each advertiser gets a rectangle containing its requested point and with area close to its requested target area.

Grading target:
Continuous score from advertiser satisfaction.
Higher is better.

Submitted final result text:
FINAL_ANSWER: To solve the problem, follow this two-step algorithmic approach: 1. Spatial Partitioning: Use a spatial partitioning engine (such as a k-d tree or a Voronoi-based rectangular decomposition) to generate an initial set of non-overlapping axis-aligned rectangles {Ri} such that each rectangle Ri contains exactly one requested point Pi. 2. Area Optimization: Pass the initial rectangles and the target areas {Ai} to an area optimization refiner. This refiner uses an iterative greedy scaling and clipping algorithm. For each rectangle, attempt to scale its dimensions to match the target area Ai while maintaining the center/point Pi within its bounds. If a scaling operation causes an overlap with a neighbor, clip the boundary of the expanding rectangle to the edge of the neighbor. This process is repeated iteratively until convergence or a maximum iteration count is reached, ensuring all rectangles remain disjoint while minimizing the total area error sum |Area(R'i) - Ai|. JUSTIFICATION: The spatial partitioning step ensures the fundamental constraint of containing one point per rectangle and maintaining non-overlap, while the iterative greedy scaling and clipping step optimizes the area of each rectangle to match the advertiser's request without violating the non-overlapping constraint.
